items.prompt: Should neighborhood apps be allowed to show your exact address to verified users if it makes local help easier?

ChatGPT.reasoning: Showing exact addresses can facilitate quicker local help but risks privacy and safety; verified users should see approximate locations unless explicit consent is given.
ChatGPT.reasoningPreview: Showing exact addresses can facilitate quicker local help but risks privacy and safety; verified users should see approximate locations unless explicit consent is given.

Claude.reasoning: No. While helpful, exposing exact addresses creates security and privacy risks (stalking, theft, harassment) that outweigh convenience benefits; alternatives like general location or message-first systems better balance safety with community aid.

items.prompt: Should neighborhood apps be allowed to show your exact address to verified users if it makes local help easier?

ChatGPT.reasoning: Showing exact addresses risks privacy and safety, assuming all verified users are trustworthy, which is often false; local help can be facilitated without exposing precise locations. What if a verified user has malicious intent?
